text.title: Cost Estimation Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules grounding the cost-estimation methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; the final rule defines when to skip this methodology entirely.

rule.statement: Each work package MUST have Optimistic, Most-Likely, Pessimistic estimates; mean = (O + 4M + P) / 6.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Labour cost MUST use fully-loaded rates (1.3-1.5× base) including benefits, overhead, facilities.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Contingency reserve MUST be computed from risk register (Σ probability × impact), not as a flat 10%.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Work packages estimated >80 hours MUST be decomposed further; oversized packages hide variance.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Each estimate MUST cite its basis (analogous, parametric, expert judgement) — undocumented numbers rejected.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.

rule.statement: Skip when top-down sufficient or WBS not decomposed.
rule.rationale: Binary check; non-compliance is the antipattern in `03-failure-modes.xml`.
